book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XXIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: They who practice divination, and follow the customs of the heathen, or who take men to their houses for the invention of sorceries, or for lustrations, fall under the canon of five years’ [penance], according to the prescribed degrees; that is, three years as prostrators, and two of prayer without oblation.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: Whoso uses vaticination and whoso introduces anyone into his house for the sake of making a poison or a lustration let him be subject to penance for five years.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: I read ἐθνῶν for χρόνων and accordingly translate “of the heathen.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Council of Ancyra.
verses.text: It is greatly to be desired that bishops and pastors to-day would take example from the fathers of Ancyra and devote their attention strenuously to eliminate superstition from the people, and would expound with animation to the people the enormity of this crime.
